title: taxanthema conspicuum
parts: Taxanthema conspicuum has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers. The roots of Taxanthema conspicuum contain medicinal constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of this plant contain compounds like terpenoids and glycosides, which have been used to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The flowers of Taxanthema conspicuum are rich in anthocyanins and flavonols, which are believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, making them useful in treating various health conditions.
side_effects: Taxanthema conspicuum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils has been linked to headaches, dizziness, and nausea. In rare cases, ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can cause stomach ulcers, diarrhea, and vomiting. Furthermore, using the plant's essential oils without proper dilution can lead to skin burns and blisters.
